con: verb. To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.
confront: verb. To stand or meet facing, especially in competition, hostility or defiance; to come face to face with.
conn: noun. The duty of directing a ship, usually used with the verb to have or to take and accompanied by the article "the."
conto: noun. In Portugal and Brazil, a million reis.
coo: noun. The murmuring sound made by a dove or pigeon.
coot: noun. Any of various aquatic birds of the genus Fulica that are mainly black with a prominent frontal shield on the forehead.
corf: noun. A large basket, especially as used for coal.
corn: noun. Any cereal plant (or its grain) that is the main crop or staple of a country or region.
cot: noun. A simple bed, especially one for portable or temporary purposes.
croft: noun. An enclosed piece of land, usually small and arable and used for small-scale food production, and often with a dwelling next to it;
croon: verb. To hum or sing (a song or tune), or to speak (words), softly in a low pitch or in a sentimental manner; specifically, to sing (a
croton: noun. Any of various plants, of the genus Croton, that yield croton oil.
font: noun. A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
foot: noun. A biological structure found in many animals that is used for locomotion and that is frequently a separate organ at the terminal part
fort: noun. A fortified defensive structure stationed with troops.
fro: adverb. From; away; back or backward.
front: noun. The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.
noon: noun. The time of day when the Sun seems to reach its highest point in the sky; solar noon.
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
onto: preposition. Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).
orc: noun. Any of several large, ferocious sea creatures, now especially the killer whale.
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
roc: noun. An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
roof: noun. The external covering at the top of a building.
root: noun. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
ton: noun. Any of various units of mass, originally notionally equal to the contents of a tun, particularly: The short ton of 2000 pounds (about
toon: noun. A cartoon, especially an animated television show.
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
coto: noun. A traditional Spanish unit of length, equivalent to about 10.4 cm.
cro: noun. Synonym of bro (“a male comrade or friend”).
crt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iyojwa'ja Chorote.
cto: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emberá-Catío.
foo: noun. Alternative form of fu: an administrative subdivision of imperial China; the capital of such divisions.
frot: verb. To rub one's penis against another person's penis for sexual gratification.
nco: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sibe.
ofo: noun. An Igbo staff of authority.
oof: interjection. A sound mimicking the loss of air, as if someone's solar plexus had just been struck.
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
orf: noun. An exanthemous disease caused by a parapox virus, occurring primarily in sheep and goats but also capable of infecting humans.
oto: noun. An otocinclus (freshwater fish, genus Otocinclus).
roon: noun. A shred or remnant (usually of cloth).
tfr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teribe.
troco: noun. Alternative form of trucco (“lawn billiards”).
tronc: noun. A monetary pool, in which tips are collected and later shared out between all staff, e.g. in a restaurant.
